author: Frank Heikens
message:
[#1196] Read complete SCRAM messages before parsing

A SCRAM message split across multiple network segments was parsed after
the first read() returned, so sasl_continue->length - 9 underflowed
(size_t) and the huge value reached the SCRAM parsers, risking a crash.
The same pattern exists in every SCRAM exchange.

Add pgmoneta_read_complete_message(), which keeps reading until the byte
count declared in the message header has arrived and rejects a header
whose declared length is malformed, and use it for the SCRAM exchanges.
Guard every SCRAM parse site against short messages and missing
attributes so a malformed message fails authentication cleanly instead
of crashing.

The invalid declared-length test exercises the rejection path, which
logs at ERROR level, so it is marked MCTF_TEST_NEGATIVE so the per-test
log-error check treats that ERROR as expected.

Counterpart of pgagroal#834.

close #1196

author: trxvorr
message:
Harden SCRAM iteration count

The SCRAM-SHA-256 iteration count from the server-first-message and the stored verifier was parsed with atoi() and used directly as the PBKDF2 loop count. atoi() ignores trailing garbage, accepts signs, and is undefined on overflow, and a large value lets a peer force unbounded PBKDF2 work.

Add scram_parse_iterations(), which accepts only a positive decimal value that fits in an int and is at most SCRAM_MAX_ITERATIONS (100000, well above PostgreSQL's default of 4096), and use it at every call site that previously parsed the count with atoi().

author: Frank Heikens
message:
[#1176] Use bounded pgmoneta_snprintf for port formatting in pgmoneta_connect

The backend port was formatted into the stack buffer sport with an
unbounded sprintf. The buffer is already correctly sized (six bytes
holds 65535 plus its NUL), so this is a consistency/hardening change,
not an overflow fix: every other formatted write in this file already
uses the bounded pgmoneta_snprintf. No behavioural change for valid
ports.

close #1176

author: Trevor Jacob Mathews
message:
[#1148] Remove md5

Performs a project-wide removal of MD5 auth. MD5 is considered insecure and its removal enforces modern authentication standards across pgmoneta.
